taxonomy: Asarca heteroglossa belongs to the phylum Apicomplexa, which is a group of protists that includes many parasites. This phylum is further divided into several classes, with Asarca heteroglossa specifically classified under the class Conoidasida. Conoidasida is a class of protists characterized by the presence of a conoid organelle in their apical complex. The apical complex is a structure found in many apicomplexan parasites that plays a crucial role in their invasion of host cells. Asarca heteroglossa is likely a type of coccidian, which is a subclass of Conoidasida.
